raw_text:
CloudVelocity Inc.

Accelerating enterprise infrastructure for modern cloud-native businesses

SENIOR DEVOPS ENGINEER

San Francisco, CA (Remote-friendly) · Full-time · $165,000 - $210,000 per year

We're seeking a Senior DevOps Engineer with 5-8 years of professional experience to join our

infrastructure team. In this role, you'll architect and maintain highly scalable Kubernetes clusters, design

infrastructure-as-code solutions using Terraform, and lead the automation of our CI/CD pipelines. You'll

mentor junior engineers, collaborate with cross-functional teams, and drive technical decisions that

impact multiple teams. This is an excellent opportunity to work on complex infrastructure challenges at

scale while taking ownership of critical production systems.

ABOUT US

CloudVelocity Inc. is a rapidly growing DevOps and cloud infrastructure consulting firm focused on

helping enterprise clients modernize their IT operations. We specialize in designing and implementing
scalable, secure, and highly available cloud infrastructure solutions across AWS and GCP. Our team of

experienced engineers works with Fortune 500 companies to automate deployments, optimize costs,
and reduce operational overhead through cutting-edge infrastructure-as-code practices.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

Design, implement, and manage production-grade Kubernetes clusters on AWS/GCP, ensuring

high availability, security, and cost optimization across multiple environments

Lead the development and maintenance of Infrastructure-as-Code solutions using Terraform,

managing version control, code reviews, and deployment pipelines for infrastructure changes

Architect and enhance CI/CD pipelines using Jenkins and GitHub Actions, enabling teams to

deploy code safely and frequently while maintaining security and compliance standards

Mentor and guide junior DevOps engineers and junior developers on cloud infrastructure best
practices, Kubernetes deployment patterns, and operational excellence

Develop and maintain comprehensive monitoring, logging, and alerting solutions using
Prometheus, ELK Stack, and cloud-native tools to ensure system reliability and rapid incident
response

Collaborate with platform, security, and application teams to define SLAs, implement disaster
recovery procedures, and conduct regular chaos engineering exercises

Automate infrastructure provisioning, scaling, and operational tasks using Python and Bash
scripting, reducing manual effort and improving system reliability

 Conduct security reviews of infrastructure configurations, implement least-privilege access

patterns, and ensure compliance with industry standards (SOC 2, HIPAA, etc.)

Lead on-call rotations, manage critical incidents, and document post-mortems to drive continuous

improvement across the infrastructure organization

REQUIRED

5-8 years of professional experience in DevOps engineering, cloud infrastructure, or systems

engineering roles

Deep expertise with Kubernetes in production environments, including cluster architecture,

networking, storage, and troubleshooting complex issues

Strong proficiency with Infrastructure-as-Code tools, specifically Terraform, with demonstrated

experience managing large-scale cloud deployments

Hands-on experience with AWS or GCP (or both), including VPC design, IAM, compute, storage,

and networking services

Advanced proficiency in Python and Bash scripting for automation, tooling, and infrastructure
management tasks

Proven experience designing and maintaining CI/CD pipelines using Jenkins, GitHub Actions, or
similar tools in high-velocity environments

Strong understanding of containerization technologies (Docker) and microservices architecture
patterns

NICE TO HAVE

Experience with multi-cloud deployments across AWS and GCP, including hybrid architecture
considerations

Demonstrated expertise in Kubernetes operators, Helm charts, and custom resource definitions
(CRDs)

Hands-on experience with service mesh technologies such as Istio or Linkerd in production
environments

Track record of optimizing cloud costs and implementing FinOps practices

Experience with infrastructure security, secrets management, and implementing GitOps workflows

TECH STACK

Kubernetes   Docker

  Terraform   AWS   GCP   GitHub Actions   Jenkins   Python   Bash   Prometheus

BENEFITS

Competitive health insurance (medical, dental, vision) with employer contribution of 90%

Unlimited paid time off (PTO) with a minimum of 15 days annually and flexible work arrangements

 Professional development budget of $3,000 per year for conferences, certifications, and training

courses

Remote-friendly work environment with modern office space in San Francisco for collaboration

401(k) retirement plan with 4% employer match and immediate vesting

Parental leave (12 weeks paid for primary caregivers, 8 weeks for non-primary caregivers)

Commuter benefits and relocation assistance for qualified candidates

Annual performance bonuses (10-15% of base salary) and stock options for eligible employees

HOW TO APPLY

Submit your resume and a cover letter highlighting your Kubernetes and Terraform experience to

careers@cloudvelocity.io. Please include links to any GitHub projects, blog posts, or public

infrastructure repositories that showcase your DevOps expertise. We review applications on a rolling

basis and typically respond within 1-2 weeks.

Demonstrated expertise in Kubernetes operators, Helm charts, and custom resource definitions
Hands-on experience with service mesh technologies such as Istio or Linkerd in production
Track record of optimizing cloud costs and implementing FinOps practices
Experience with infrastructure security, secrets management, and implementing GitOps workflows
